title: Bakery Mezzanine Rack-Gantry Sizing
strategies: trivial: uniform a_max sizing (evaluator baseline, always feasible) -> ~0.1

greedy: single-pass fully-stressed design (area = |force|/sigma) from the uniform analysis; because the frame is X-braced and statically indeterminate the load redistributes toward the stiffened members and their stress climbs back over yield, and the sag gate is ignored -> infeasible (0) on most instances

strong: damped iterated fully-stressed design that drives the indeterminate force redistribution to a fixed point (with a yield safety margin) + uniform sag-gate repair; feasible on both gates and far lighter than baseline
invalid: uniform a_min sizing -> stresses exceed yield everywhere -> 0
notes: Feasibility-gated-structural (Frontier-Eng StructuralOptimization) skinned as a bakery-warehouse mezzanine rack gantry. Novel variant vs the determinate trellis: every panel is X-braced, so the 2D pin-jointed truss is STATICALLY INDETERMINATE and member forces depend on the chosen cross-sections, defeating a single-pass fully-stressed design. Pure-numpy FEM (direct stiffness) in the parent; candidate is an isolated stdin->stdout program via isorun. Score = min(1, 0.1*b/weight), infeasible->0; per-instance sag-gate tightness keeps strong below 1.0.
